tweets.full_text:
A lawyer would never trust AI to defend him in court.
A surgeon won’t let it hold the scalpel.
A general won’t hand it the launch codes. Not willingly at least. 
The people selling you the “AI replaces everything” story have one thing in common.
None of them are offering to be replaced.

Sam Altman talks about AI displacing entire professions. 
What he leaves out is the part where the tools cost money, require infrastructure, demand technical literacy, and still hallucinate in ways that would get a human fired on the first day.

The jobs AI actually threatens are the ones already hanging by a thread. 
Entry level. Precarious. Low margin. 
The people with the least runway to absorb the hit.

The lawyer keeps his job. The paralegal doesn’t.
The executive keeps his job. The analyst doesn’t.
The doctor keeps his job. The radiologist reading routine scans might not.

But here’s where it gets interesting.

The paralegal who doesn’t get hired today was supposed to become the lawyer in fifteen years.

That pipeline just got cut.

The intern class that never came in. 
The junior associate who never got thrown into the grinder. 
The entry level researcher who got replaced before they even learned the craft.

They don’t disappear quietly, they  just never arrive.

And when the current generation of lawyers, surgeons, and engineers ages out, retires, or dies, who replaces them?

The apprentices who never apprenticed.
The juniors who were never hired.
The humans who were made redundant?

So by default, AI gets the seat at the table.

Not because it earned it.
Not because it passed the bar, survived residency, or spent a decade learning how things actually fail in the real world.

But because there’s nobody else left.
And that’s when the bill comes due.

An AI that hallucinates case law defending someone’s life in court. 
A diagnostic system optimizing for liability rather than the patient in front of it. 
Infrastructure managed by models that have never experienced the consequences of being wrong.

They call it progress.

It’s actually a slow motion handover of critical systems to tools that have no skin in the game, no accountability when they fail, no capacity to care about the difference, and are fully controlled by billionaires who never gave a flying f about you to begin with. 

The disruption was never the point but the  dependency was.

They don’t need to replace you.
They just need to sit between you and everything you need.

Your doctor. Your lawyer. Your bank. Your government. Your health. 

Not eliminated.

Gated. Gate-kept. 
Pay the toll or wait in the queue.
That’s the business model.

tweets.full_text:
I recently came across a Web3 ecosystem that is taking a very different approach to token launch infrastructure.

@clarnium_io is building a modular ecosystem designed to connect token creation, community growth, AI automation, and on-chain participation into a single experience.

What caught my attention is that they are not focused on launching tokens alone.

They are trying to solve everything that comes after the launch.

• ClarFun serves as a no-code token launchpad on Base, allowing anyone to create a token in a few clicks while automatically migrating liquidity to Aerodrome once a token reaches a $69,000 market cap.

• AI Agents powered by Grok and X API help creators generate content, build token websites, automate social campaigns, and maintain community engagement at scale.

• ClarPay serves as their enterprise-grade multichain payment gateway which has already successfully processed over five million dollars in transaction volume.

• DYORs Base makes exploring Web3 a lot easier, with a growing database covering 1,000+ games, tokens, and ecosystem insights all in one place.

• The Telegram Games Hub and Profile ID remove a lot of the usual onboarding friction, bringing Web2 and Web3 together while letting users earn rewards simply by staying active.

One of the more interesting mechanics is the incentive model.

Creators receive 30% of ClarFun trading fees and 30% of migration fees when their token successfully reaches Aerodrome.

That creates a direct connection between platform activity and creator rewards.

The ecosystem also incorporates fair launch principles with no team token allocations, automatic liquidity migration, LP token burning, and transparent on-chain participation mechanics.

Beyond the products themselves, Clarnium is building an Alpha DAO structure powered by $CLAR, giving the community tier-based access to ecosystem opportunities while helping surface promising launches through the ClarFun AI Grant Program.

Most launchpads help users create tokens.

Clarnium is trying to build the infrastructure that helps those tokens grow, attract attention, and sustain activity after launch.

That feels much closer to where Web3 infrastructure is heading.

Curious to explore the ecosystem yourself?
🔗 Join here: https://t.co/af1ruKoqOA

tweets.full_text:
🇦🇲 Armenia launches one of the world’s most advanced AI data centers

Armenia has officially joined the ranks of countries with their own artificial intelligence infrastructure. #EleveightAI has announced the launch of the #SouthCaucasus’ first AI Factory in the town of Gagarin, powered by #NVIDIA Blackwell B300 chips — one of the world’s most advanced architectures for generative AI.

The project is now valued at up to $120 million for the first phase, significantly above the previously reported $70 million. The facility is designed to scale up to 35 MW of computing capacity and is considered supercomputing-class infrastructure.

According to the company, Armenia has the potential to become a new global hub for #AI computing — an alternative location to the United States and Western #Europe. Among the advantages cited are lower energy costs, the availability of technical talent, and the region’s growing international connectivity.

Eleveight AI CEO Arman Aleksanyan stated that the goal of the project is to transform Armenia from a consumer of AI into a country where AI is developed, trained, and deployed. The company is also considering future expansion into #CentralAsia'n and European markets.

Twenty percent of the center’s computing capacity will be provided free of charge to Armenian universities, research institutions, and non-profit organizations.

The project is already being described as part of a broader strategy to turn #Armenia into a regional technology and AI hub. As part of that strategy, another AI data center — developed by FireBird — is expected to launch in #Hrazdan, with planned investments of $500 million in the first phase and $4 billion in the second.

NVIDIA Blackwell B300 chips are considered among the most advanced AI processors in the world and are designed for generative AI, supercomputing, and high-performance computing workloads. Access to such technology is subject to #US export-control regulations and is granted under strict licensing and compliance requirements.

#IT #technology #MiddleEast #EU

tweets.full_text:
On May 28, Jorge R. Gutierrez, creator of El Tigre: The Adventures of Manny Rivera, announced a new AI-generated cartoon called Punky Duck as part of Amazon’s AI Creators’ Fund.

The next day, Guillermo del Toro posted messages celebrating animation made by real artists, including the line “Made by humans for humans.” Many in the animation community took it as a jab at the AI project, and backlash built quickly.

By May 30, Gutierrez had pulled out of the program. In a public apology, he said his intention was to spotlight the artists working on the project, not replace them, and promised to do better moving forward.

tweets.full_text:
日々進化するAIのリリース情報など収集してると、もう愕然とするというか、プロンプト入力して丸投げすれば、勝手に映像作って編集までしてくれる機能が搭載されたりして、なんだかやる気を無くす。

が、人工知能に負けてたまるか！という想いで、常に頭の中で試行錯誤、ジェミニや、grokと対話しながら、自分にしかできない独自の肉体表現との融合、実際にある場所などを使った映像創りや、追求は変えたくない。

という事で、そう、
決して"架空の場所"ではなく、いつものお気に入りのカフェで、注文したアイスコーヒーと、トーストを目の前に起き、先ずは一枚撮る。

そして、
向かいの白い椅子に、
誰かを座らせる事ができるか❓

検証。

何度もプロンプト修正、
ようやく理想通りの画作りができた。

この白い椅子に、
想像超えた人物達が集結した‼️

さぁ、誰だと思う❓

tweets.full_text:
Claude Code × Dynamic Workflows、えぐい。

Anthropicが開発したAIコーディングツール「Claude Code」の開発者が、新機能「動的ワークフロー」を使って数百件の作業を一気に片付けた、というのが海外で話題に。

・プロンプトに「workflow」と書くだけでAIが自動で実行計画を作成
・数百のAIエージェントをまたいでも順番通りに処理が進む
・1件ずつではなく並行処理で、まとめて一気に終わらせられる

など、「何をどの順番でやるか」をAI自身に設計させる仕組みです👇
